message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "high-enchantment sword (enchantment 5) adds 30% surcharge to that item's base"
Expected: 145
Current error: received 115
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Add high enchantment surcharge: when item.enchantment >= 5, add 30% of item base to item subtotal.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.

Now I understand. The test expects 145 for a sword with enchantment 5. Calculation: 100 base + 30 (30% surcharge) + 13 (10% first insurance on 130) + 5 fee = 148... wait let me recompute.

Looking at the cursed test: 100 + 50 (curse) = 150 item subtotal, plus 10 first insurance (10% of 100 base) + 5 fee = 165. So first insurance is on base, not on item subtotal.

For enchantment 5: 100 + 30 (high-ench) = 130 item subtotal, + 10 first insurance (10% of 100 base) + 5 fee = 145. That matches.

I need to add: when item.enchantment >= 5, add 30% of item base to itemSubtotal.

input.old_string: export function quote(input: { items: Array<{ type: string; cursed?: boolean }> }): { premium: number } {
input.new_string: export function quote(input: { items: Array<{ type: string; cursed?: boolean; enchantment?: number }> }): { premium: number } {

content.text:
All 14 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added high-enchantment surcharge in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_22-41-54_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts` — when an item has `enchantment >= 5`, add 30% of its base premium to the item subtotal.
**Result**: All 14 active tests pass (36 todos remain).
**Approach**: Minimal change — extended the existing item-subtotal accumulation line with a parallel ternary mirroring the existing `cursed` surcharge pattern, and added `enchantment?: number` to the input type to satisfy TypeScript.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
